description_long: This is a modern military attack helicopter, resembling the AH-64 Apache, with a sleek, angular fuselage painted in matte black. The helicopter features a tandem cockpit with large, multi-pane windows, a prominent sensor suite above the main rotor, and stub wings equipped with weapon pylons. The main rotor has four long, narrow blades with yellow tips, while the tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and a four-bladed tail rotor mounted on the port side. The landing gear consists of robust, tubular skids with crossbars for stability. Various antennas, sensors, and armaments are visible, emphasizing its combat-ready design.
description: A matte black military attack helicopter with a tandem cockpit, four-bladed main and tail rotors, and robust landing skids. Features include weapon pylons, sensor arrays, and angular fuselage details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, main and tail rotors, tandem cockpit, and weapon pylons.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the cockpit windows, sensor suite above the rotor, and symmetrical stub wings with mounted armaments.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the tail rotor and landing skids.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids and stabilizers.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow main rotor blades, likely composite material, with yellow tips and a matte black finish. The blades are straight and evenly spaced around the central hub.
attribute_tail_rotor: Four-bladed tail rotor mounted vertically on the port side of the tail boom, with black blades and a compact, angular hub. No visible markings.
attribute_landing_skids: Tubular, matte black landing skids with crossbars for structural support, made of metal, and extending the length of the lower fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, angular cockpit windows arranged in a tandem configuration, with dark tint and black framing. The windows are multi-pane and wrap around the front and sides of the cockpit.
